evals.prompt: Map all reachable surfaces of my local web app at http://localhost:3000. Use headed desktop mode at 2560x1440 only, no mobile. Build a long manifest of routes, dialogs, menus, forms, tables, tabs, and state variants, then run coverage so every discovered surface ends as PASS, FAIL, BLOCKED, or SKIPPED.
evals.expected_output: Creates a surface manifest, uses desktop-only headed browser setup, enumerates sub-surfaces instead of only pages, and reports explicit coverage gaps without pretending unreachable states were tested.

evals.prompt: Dogfood my authenticated dashboard and make the surface list exhaustive. I want every top-nav section, row action menu, confirm dialog, filter state, and empty/error state captured in a single coverage model for desktop at 2560x1440. Then adversarially test the highest-risk flows.
evals.expected_output: Uses dogfood-style discovery, ui-test-style structured accounting, and expect-style adversarial prompts; records parent-child surface relationships and marks blocked states honestly.

evals.prompt: Do a desktop-only exhaustive interaction pass on my app. I do not want mobile for now. Create a very long list of all app surfaces first, then test every discovered interaction and give me a coverage summary with missing surfaces called out explicitly.
evals.expected_output: Prioritizes surface inventory before execution, avoids mobile checks, uses 2560x1440 headed mode, and outputs a final coverage summary with total discovered surfaces and unresolved gaps.
